structure.affirmative: Subject + V1 (+ -s/-es for he/she/it) — She works. / They work.
structure.negative: Subject + don't/doesn't + V1 — She doesn't work. / They don't work.
structure.question: Do/Does + Subject + V1? — Does she work? / Do they work?

faq.a: Add -s/-es only after He / She / It: 'She works'. Use the base form for I / You / We / They: 'They work'.

faq.q: Is 'I am going to…' Present Simple?
faq.a: No, 'am going to' is a future construction. Present Simple is used for future only with timetables: 'The bus leaves at 8'.

faq.q: Does 'I live in Moscow' describe a habit or a fact?
faq.a: It's a permanent state — use Present Simple. Habits (repeated actions) and states (unchanging facts) both use Present Simple.
